metadata.title:
WOMEN INSPECT NEW CLUB


HOME WITH DELIGHT.

pages.raw_text:
WOMGN INotEOt NEW OlO6


HOME WITH DELIGHT.


The new home of the Woman's
club on Ninth and Walnut was
open to inspection yesterday, and
fully $50 women from the differ
ent clubs of the city were there to
comment rapturously upon the ap.
pointments Of Spokane's first
woman's club house. Downtown
firms were very generous in their
aid. number contributing articles
of furniture, etc., and the result is
an exceedingly attractive and well
appointed club cottage. There Is q
miniature stage for elocution and
lecture entertainments, and the
basement is fitted up as kitchen
and dining room.


X. OF c. OFFICERS ELECTED.


Officers for the coming year
were elected at the meeting Of the
Spokane council No. 683, Knights
of Columbus, last night. F. j.
Dorsey was elected grand knight
and Lester P. Edge deputy grand
knight. e. j. Kane was chosen
financial and ]. T. Skerrett record
ing secretary and Guy M. Coffey,
treasurer.


BAZAAR PLANS BEING COM


PlETED.


The Christmas bazaar to be
given by the Ladies' Benevolent
society, beginning Monday, is to be
one of the largest affairs of it's ma
ture given this season. Everything
imaginable, from gents furnish
ings to lemonade and peanuts will
be displayed to the best advantage
and the booths will be beautifully
decorated and attractively fur
nished.


PLAN BRIDGE PARTY IN NEW


HOME.


Members of the WOman's club
will play at bridge in their new
home Thursday evening. Hand
painted sketches of the home will
be used as souvenir score cards.
A musical program will proceed the
card games.


K. OF Prs WILL CELEBRATE
NINETEENTH ANNIVERSARY.


The Knights of Pythias will eele-
brate the nineteenth anniversary
of their founding on Thursday. A
program followed with a dance, in
GLIDING old-fashioned steps, will be
given in the Pacific hall. Tonight
the sisters will entertain at one of
their dances.
